schema_notes: Per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual deep research — Henry Kravis single-subject run + regen_v3@sha256-4fc43709be8c748d

person.wealth_source: KKR & Co. co-founder (1976) and co-executive chairman; pioneer of leveraged buyouts. Wealth is concentrated in KKR stock plus decades of fund carry distributions.
person.spouse: Marie-Josée Kravis (economist; MoMA board chair). Most major gifts are made jointly through 'The Marie-Josée and Henry R. Kravis Foundation.'

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 (March): $11.5B, rank #275, down from $13.3B on the 2025 list (#176). GuruFocus KKR-share-count estimate puts insider-visible holdings ~$10.2B. QuiverQuant shows only Form-4-reported stake of ~$1.1B; differs because most KKR stake held via trusts/LLCs not attributed on insider forms. Forbes remains canonical denominator.

sources.note: Direct fetch blocked in session; value corroborated by Palm Beach Post (2026-03-27) reporting Forbes 2026 figure of $11.5B, down from $13.3B.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: KKR is publicly traded; Kravis's stake is large but not company-dominant the way Ellison's Oracle stake is. He is 82 and stepped down as co-CEO in Oct 2021 (now co-executive chairman), so career carry distributions have largely been monetized. Apply ~55% liquidity — higher than a founder-CEO but lower than pure cash — for 'giving capacity now' calculations.

cited_events.evidence: Direct fetch of https://www.givingpledge.org/pledger/henry-kravis/ returned 404. Wikipedia's Giving Pledge signer list and givingpledge.org/about do not include Henry Kravis or Marie-Josée Kravis. Institutional Investor's 2010-era coverage of Giving Pledge expansion specifically cites Kravis's Columbia gift but does NOT list him as a Giving Pledge signer. No news item from 2010-2026 found describing a Kravis Giving Pledge signing.

sources.result: Article on Giving Pledge expansion names Kravis's Columbia gift as context but does NOT name him as a pledger

cited_events.correction_note: IMPORTANT CORRECTION TO TASK BRIEF: The initial task stated Kravis 'SIGNED (June 2010)' the Giving Pledge. Based on direct verification against givingpledge.org (404 on Kravis profile URL), Wikipedia's signer list, and the givingpledge.org/about page, this appears FALSE. Kravis does not appear to be a Giving Pledge signer. He is a prolific observable giver, but his philanthropy is structured through a family foundation, not a public pledge commitment. This materially changes the tier analysis: with NO pledge, there can be no 'pledge breach' penalty, only observation vs expected.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Lowest-confidence floor from USA Today tracking (~$1.2M combined Kravis + Roberts media-owner donations 2010s-2020s). Individual major event: $1M to Trump 2017 inauguration. 1997 Republican Leadership Council co-founder (with Eisenberg). Multiple Republican presidential campaigns since Bush 41. FEC API direct-pull not yet run; floor likely understated. Classification is 'moderate' relative to Ellison-scale — Kravis has not bankrolled single super-PACs at $15M+ levels.

sources.note: Combined Kravis + Roberts total 2010s-2020s as media owners (Axel Springer / Politico / Business Insider context)
Co-founded Republican Leadership Council 1997 with Lewis Eisenberg; supported Bush 41, Bush 43, McCain
Historical snapshot: Kravis personally gave $0 to Democrats, $5K to Lieberman (I), $70.8K to Republicans in one cycle (cycle unspecified on source — needs verification)

foundations_active.name: The Marie-Josée and Henry R. Kravis Foundation
foundations_active.state: Originally NY; currently registered in FL (West Palm Beach / Miami)
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (Henry R. Kravis — Chairman; Marie-Josée Kravis — President; Lewis M. Eisenberg — Treasurer; James Goldrick — Secretary; Paul E. Raether — Treasurer 2017-2019. All zero-compensation per ProPublica summary.)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants $45.4M vs end-year assets $68.2M = 66% — well above 5% legal minimum. Historic pattern: foundation runs LOW-ASSET, HIGH-FLOW — 2019 EoY assets $7.8M vs grants $72.7M indicates the foundation is refilled annually by Kravis contributions rather than operating from a permanent endowment. No LOW_PAYOUT flag.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED — full-filing recipient list parse pending.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity identified in summary data. Absence-of-evidence only. Full 990-PF recipient parse pending.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC (unlike CZI or Emerson Collective). All observable charitable giving flows through MJHKF 501(c)(3) private foundation.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.
detected_vehicles.trusts_note: Significant share of Kravis's KKR stake is held via trusts/LLCs (GuruFocus shows ~$10.2B insider-visible, QuiverQuant shows ~$1.1B in personal Form-4 reporting — the gap implies substantial trust holdings). Structure (dynasty? GRAT? CLT? revocable?) not publicly disclosed. Flag for follow-up.

red_flags_not_present: GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (Kravis did NOT sign the Giving Pledge — no breach possible; also no pledge credit.)
LOW_PAYOUT (MJHKF 2024 payout ~66% of assets; foundation runs hot, refilled annually.)
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (All officers zero comp per ProPublica summary.)
NO_OBSERVABLE_GIVING (Observable giving ~$620M foundation grants_out 2011-2024 is ~5.4% of current net worth — above Bezos/Ellison/Zuckerberg by ratio, below Gates/Buffett/Scott.)
DAF_TRANSFERS (not confirmed absent — full recipient parse pending, but summary pattern is direct-to-named-institutions)

potential_red_flags_to_investigate.evidence: Kravis's KKR has major oil & gas portfolio; he gave $1M to Trump's 2017 inauguration. MoMA climate protesters have specifically targeted the couple over this. Not a philanthropy red flag per se; noted for context.

MJHKF 2019 EoY assets $7.8M vs grants $72.7M — foundation is a pass-through vehicle, not a perpetual endowment. Not illegal (most modern LBO-era foundations run this way) but means 'observable giving' year-to-year equals fresh Kravis transfers in; there is no committed endowment corpus growing to cover the residual life-giving he has implied via naming gifts.

rollup.observable_giving_method: Sum of MJHKF grants_out from ProPublica summary years 2011-2024 (14 years with available data): $14.3M + $21.0M + $21.6M + $38.8M + $46.2M + [2016 missing — est $35M] + $45.2M + $47.3M + $72.7M + $25.9M + $34.4M + $56.1M + $55.2M + $45.4M = $623.9M (including $35M estimate for unparsed 2016). Excludes: Trump 2017 political $1M; related family foundations; pre-1997 direct gifts (MSK wing $10M 1987, Mount Sinai $10M 1988) which total ~$20M if added back.
observable_giving_todo: Pull 2016 MJHKF 990-PF PDF to fill the one missing year.
Pull 2006-2010 MJHKF 990-PF PDFs (pre-ProPublica-summary window) to capture Columbia Business School $100M pledge disbursement schedule.
Parse full 2024 filing at /202532889349100103/full for recipient list — confirm no DAF grants, extract any directed-giving structure.
FEC API pull to tighten political_giving floor beyond press-cited $1.2M + $1M inauguration.
Reconcile $10M vs $75M Mount Sinai figure in task brief — actual founding gift was $10M per Wikipedia; no verified $75M Kravis gift to Mount Sinai found.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.55 × $11.5B = $6.325B; tenure cap saturated (30 years). Expected = $632.5M.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked. Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Kravis is 82, net worth $11.5B. Apply modest 0.25% of liquid wealth as cohort upper: 0.0025 × $6.325B = $15.8M annual → rough 2-year window upper $30M.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Kravis is Jewish (Tulsa childhood Temple Israel). Small synagogue-giving window possible but not material at this scale.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: GuruFocus/QuiverQuant gap implies ~$9B of KKR stock held via trusts/LLCs not on Form 4. If dynasty-structured, charitable outcome is ~0. If CLT/CRT, could eventually disburse significant residual to MJHKF. Set envelope at $2B — representing a plausible ~20% charitable-remainder tranche — as confidence-interval visibility. Do NOT include in tier calc.

rollup.tier_rule: Kravis observable giving ($624M) is ~99% of the strict 10%-of-liquid-wealth-tenure-weighted norm ($632M). He has NOT signed the Giving Pledge, so 'pledge breach' penalty does not apply. MJHKF runs at 66% payout, zero officer comp, no DAF red flag. This is the strongest observable-giving-to-expected ratio of any Kravis-tier billionaire in the scrooge cohort thus far. Tier C 'On Track' reflects: public, auditable, consistent giving at roughly the formal 10% norm — but below the Tier D 'over-giver' threshold (Gates, Buffett, Scott, Soros who give multiples of their net worth).
rollup.tier_published_caveat: The headline reading: Kravis gives more observably than most mega-rich peers, through a family foundation that is visible on 990-PF filings. Limits of this reading: (a) foundation is a pass-through with low endowment — if Kravis stops contributing, grants stop; (b) ~$9B of his KKR stake is held via opaque trust/LLC structures whose charitable disposition is unknown; (c) he did NOT sign the Giving Pledge and has made no public multi-decade total-fortune commitment; (d) political giving and fossil-fuel investment context make him a polarizing figure despite strong observable philanthropy.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula. giving_gap = (1 - 0.986) × 40 = 0.56. tenure = max at 30 years. pledge_breach = 0 (did not sign). red_flag = 2.5 (low-severity flag for low-endowment foundation). DO NOT PUBLISH — tier only.
